steps.title: Identify the tense requirement
steps.content: The phrase “he upheld for his remaining six years in office” uses the past tense verb “upheld” to describe a completed historical period, indicating that the blank verb must also be past tense to maintain consistency.

steps.title: Choose the past tense verb
steps.content:
Verb tense consistency requires all main verbs in a narrative to use the same tense for completed events. “Vowed” is the correct past tense verb expressing Roosevelt's commitment when he returned from the excursion.

The other three options violate this principle by using non-past tenses rather than past tense.

This matches the historical narrative and establishes Roosevelt's vow as a completed action from his presidency.

That confirms B is correct.
